title: Watching Rebuild 101 for the first time
display_title: Watching Rebuild 101 for the first time
wikitext:
[]: Watching Rebuild 1.01 for the first time             Quote Post by SilentOne » Tue Jan 18, 2011 11:33 am I'm not new to the Eva series at all- I love Evangelion. However, I kind of got out of the loop for a while and, today, I just happened to decide to watch Rebuild. I apologize if this has been discussed before on here, but there didn't appear to be any single topic on the issue, so my question is this: Sachiel is designated as the Fourth Angel in Rebuild, while he was designated Third in the original series. Adam, Lilith, and then Sachiel. Did I miss something or has there been another angel added before Sachiel and, possibly, Lilith?

[]: Re: Watching Rebuild 1.01 for the first time             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jan 18, 2011 12:20 pm SilentOne wrote: Did I miss something or has there been another angel added before Sachiel and, possibly, Lilith?

[]: Re: Watching Rebuild 1.01 for the first time             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jan 18, 2011 1:32 pm Kendrix wrote: He was nicknamed 'Tunneliel' by the fandom because, well, said prologue involves Tunnels.

[]:             Quote Post by SilentOne » Tue Jan 18, 2011 1:32 pm Ohhhh, okay. Thanks for the clarification!

[]: Re: Watching Rebuild 1.01 for the first time             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Jan 18, 2011 1:47 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: And because Bone-iel sounds stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by SilentOne » Tue Jan 18, 2011 2:34 pm I remember watching the clip where Mari fought the underground angel, but it didn't even dawn on me that it was a flashback, so. :P Just finished Rebuild 1. Thought it was alright, albeit the dialogue a bit superfluous in places. Now I gotta find somewhere to watch Rebuild 2 with subs. ;-;

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 19, 2011 6:55 am SilentOne wrote: I remember watching the clip where Mari fought the underground angel, but it didn't even dawn on me that it was a flashback, so. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jan 19, 2011 12:17 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Not a flashback. The Third Angel had apparently been kept inside that facility for some time (maybe even years), ever since it was found in an embryonic state somewhere inside a glacier or something. The fight was its attempt to escape. For reference, Kaji flies out of there just before all hell breaks loose, and he carries the armoured suitcase that he delivers to Gendo later, when he arrives in Tokyo-3. So, not a flashback.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jan 19, 2011 2:15 pm This would be correct. The Angels are numbered in the order they are discovered, not in the order in which they wreak havoc/are killed. It's very clear that Tunniel had been in captivity for quite a while, and may be an allusion to Shamshel of the original.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 19, 2011 2:38 pm Sachi wrote: This would be correct. The Angels are numbered in the order they are discovered, not in the order in which they wreak havoc/are killed. It's very clear that Tunniel had been in captivity for quite a while, and may be an allusion to Shamshel of the original.
